fact: August 27 sits exactly 239 days into the year, which means there are only 126 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for monarch butterflies to start their epic 3,000-mile journey south to Mexico - some of the longest migrations in the insect world!
The sun rises about 2 minutes later than it did just a week ago, and darkness creeps in faster each evening as Earth tilts away from summer.
August 27 falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, making it one of the most 'perfectionist' days of the year according to astrology fans.
In many northern forests, maple trees start pumping out their first tiny hints of red and orange pigments right around now, even though leaves won't change for weeks.
This date sits exactly 2 months before Halloween and 4 months before Christmas - making it the perfect 'countdown calendar' starting point for holiday planning!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, August 27 marks the final stretch of winter, with spring flowers like wattle trees getting ready to explode in yellow blooms.
The moon phase on August 27 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it creates some of the most spectacular 'harvest moon' colors - deep orange and red.
Late August is when many spiders reach their full adult size and start building their biggest, most impressive webs to catch migrating insects.
In northern regions, this is peak time for wild berries like blackberries and elderberries to ripen, turning forest floors into natural candy stores.
August 27 falls during the 'dog days of summer' aftermath, when morning dew starts getting heavier and more frequent as humidity builds up overnight.
The Earth is moving through space at 67,000 miles per hour on this date, just like every other day, but we're also getting closer to the autumn equinox with each passing hour.
